title: Context layering wiring
purpose: All context infrastructure is built but nothing is connected. This brief wires context file loading, rule directory scanning, and mtime staleness detection into build_runtime. It assembles the base system instruction in the correct layering order and verifies that compaction survival works via the existing split-prompt architecture.
task: In norn-cli's build_runtime: load always-on context files via the context loader, scan rule directories and populate the RuleEngine, assemble system_sections[0] in the correct order (Norn base prompt, profile instructions, user NORN.md, project NORN.md, skill catalog listing). Wire mtime staleness checking into the iteration loop. Verify that always-on context survives compaction (part of system_sections[0]) and that active rules re-activate via presence tracking.

requirements.title: Context file loading in build_runtime
requirements.spec: WHEN build_runtime executes, it SHALL construct a context loader, load always-on NORN.md files, and store the loader on LoopContext for staleness checking.
acceptance: build_runtime calls context::loader to load ~/.norn/NORN.md and {cwd}/NORN.md
The context loader is stored on LoopContext (accessible for mtime checks)
Missing context files do not cause build_runtime to fail

requirements.title: Rule directory scanning in build_runtime
requirements.spec: WHEN build_runtime executes, it SHALL scan rule directories and add all parsed rules to the RuleEngine on LoopContext.
acceptance: build_runtime calls context::scanner to scan {cwd}/.norn/rules/ and ~/.norn/rules/
All successfully parsed rules are added to LoopContext.rules via add_rule()
Parse failures for individual rule files are logged but do not block startup

requirements.title: Base instruction layering order
requirements.spec: system_sections[0] SHALL be assembled in this order: (1) Norn base prompt from build_system_prompt(), (2) profile system_instructions, (3) user-level NORN.md content, (4) project-root NORN.md content, (5) skill catalog listing (when norn-skills is wired).
acceptance: The base instruction contains the Norn identity line before any NORN.md content
Profile instructions appear before NORN.md content
User NORN.md content appears before project NORN.md content
When no context files exist, the order is base prompt + profile instructions only

requirements.title: Dynamic sections contain rules and prompt commands
requirements.spec: system_sections[1..] SHALL contain only dynamic content: prompt command outputs, rule injections, nested NORN.md injections, and skill bodies. These sections SHALL be cleared and rebuilt each iteration.
acceptance: After clear_dynamic_sections(), system_sections has length 1 (base only)
Rule injections appear in system_sections[1..] after process_event()
Prompt command outputs appear in system_sections[1..] after evaluate_prompt_commands()

requirements.title: Compaction survival verification
requirements.spec: Always-on context files SHALL survive compaction because they are part of system_sections[0]. Active rules SHALL re-activate after compaction via the RuleEngine's presence tracking.
acceptance: After simulated compaction (clear_dynamic_sections), system_sections[0] still contains NORN.md content
After compaction, a PathChanged event re-triggers a previously-active rule
No special compaction logic is added — the existing split-prompt architecture handles this

requirements.title: Mtime check wiring
requirements.spec: The agent loop's iteration setup SHALL call the context loader's staleness check at the start of each iteration. WHEN staleness is detected, system_sections[0] SHALL be rebuilt.
acceptance: The staleness check runs alongside clear_dynamic_sections and evaluate_prompt_commands
A modified NORN.md file is picked up within one iteration
The mtime check adds no more than two stat() syscalls per iteration

requirements.title: Clean lint and format gates
requirements.spec: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings SHALL pass clean. cargo fmt --check SHALL pass clean.
acceptance: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings exits 0
cargo fmt --check exits 0

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the context module internals (types, loader, scanner) — only wire them
SHALL NOT modify the rules engine internals — only populate it via add_rule()
SHALL NOT modify the rules parser — NX-003 handles format compatibility
SHALL NOT run in parallel with NS-006 (both modify builder.rs)
verification: cargo test -p norn -p norn-cli passes all tests
A test NORN.md at project root appears in the system prompt (integration test)
A test rule in .norn/rules/ activates when a matching file is read (integration test)
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
